pi_items.source: setup_task.sh confirms it copies or downloads the dataset to this path.
export_result.sh checks for the existence of this file to compute the score.
export_result.sh reads from this file to verify the required statistics.
verifier.py checks for 'anxiety', 'gender', 'f', 'p', and 'mean' in the report.
Statistical definitions bound p-values and eta-squared to [0, 1]. Exam score range limits means to [0, 100]. Custom python script confirmed actual values fall in these bounds.

privileged_info_summary: The task asks the agent to perform an ANCOVA on the synthetic 'Exam Anxiety' dataset (from Andy Field, 2013). Executing a python-based ANCOVA (Exam ~ Anxiety + Gender) independently verifies the expected statistics: the Anxiety covariate effect is significant (F ≈ 24.1, p ≈ 0.000003); the main effect of Gender is non-significant (F ≈ 0.004, p ≈ 0.949, η² ≈ 0.00003); marginal means are approximately 56.7 (Male) and 56.4 (Female). The homogeneity of regression slopes (interaction) test yields p ≈ 0.330. The expected output file paths and report requirements from the task metadata are confirmed to match the verifier scripts.
